title: Unable to use nmcli inside podman container
body:
Dear podman team,
I tried to replicate docker solution to podman in order to be able to use nmcli
Thus I have this

```
$ podman run --name qcow --net host  --privileged  -v /var/run/dbus:/var/run/dbus -it fedora:36 /bin/bash
# dnf -y install nmstate ks-install pykickstart qemu virt-install virt-viewer  buildah podman NetworkManager
#  nmstatectl version
nmstatectl 2.1.0
#  nmstatectl show
NmstateError: Bug: DbusConnectionError: D-Bus handshake failed: Unexpected server AUTH reply dbus: Some(Handshake("Unexpected server AUTH reply"))
# nmcli 
Error: Could not create NMClient object: Exhausted all available authentication mechanisms (tried: EXTERNAL) (available: EXTERNAL).
``` 

Thanks for your help

Best regards

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out, @bioinfornatics!

Please make sure to fill out the entire issue template next time. This will speed up triaging as we don't have to ask for environment data and the version of Podman etc.

Regarding your issue: Can you try running podman as root?  Docker on your machine is likely running with root privileges and mounting `/var/run/dbus` looks suspicious to me.

Correct unless a unprivileged user can talk to /var/run/dbus this is likely not going to work.

As far as the dbus server is concerned it it talking to your rootless user not UID==0.

Thanks a lot @vrothberg and @rhatdan ,
I can confirm that we are able to use nmcli when the user own the `UID 0`.
Is is possible to do the same things in rootless ?

Thanks a lot,
Have a good day

**Note:**
```
# podman --version
podman version 3.4.4
```

> Is is possible to do the same things in rootless ?

If the rootless user can access /var/run/dbus, then the rootless container can as well.  In other words, a rootless container cannot gain more privileges than the user it is running under.

On my Fedora 36 system, `/var/run/dbus` has 0755 so it should be accessible to all users. However, it has the `system_u:object_r:system_dbusd_var_run_t:s0` SELinux context.  Running the container with `--security-opt label=disable` works for me.

I am going to close the issue but we can continue the conversation here.
